_meta.description: Focus areas selectable by paid users to deepen their scan. Each focus area unlocks additional checks and a specific set of follow-up questions in the wizard.
_meta.usage: On the paid wizard (Step 2), users select 1–3 focus areas. The scanner runs all free checks + the paid checks tagged to the selected areas. The follow-up questions shown in the Step 3 modal are filtered to match.

focus_areas.label: GDPR & EU/UK Data Protection
focus_areas.short_desc: Are you meeting GDPR requirements — and can you prove it to UK and EU enterprise clients?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if any of your clients or learners are UK or EU-based, or if you've been asked about GDPR compliance in a procurement process.
follow_up_questions: Do you have clients or learners based in the UK or EU?
Have you registered with the ICO (or equivalent data protection authority)?
Do you have a named person responsible for data protection in your business?
When did you last review your privacy policy?
Have any clients asked you to sign a Data Processing Agreement?
focus_areas.output_intro: Based on your website and your answers, here's where you stand on GDPR requirements that enterprise UK and EU clients will check:

focus_areas.label: CCPA & US Privacy
focus_areas.short_desc: Do you meet California consumer privacy requirements for US-based clients?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you have US clients or learners, particularly in California.
follow_up_questions: Do you have clients or learners based in the US?
Do you use any tools that place advertising or analytics cookies on US visitors?
Do you sell or share personal data with any third parties for marketing purposes?
focus_areas.output_intro: Here's how your website and practices align with key US privacy expectations:

focus_areas.label: Enterprise Procurement Readiness
focus_areas.short_desc: Would your website and documents pass the checks that large employers run on L&D suppliers?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you're pitching to large employers, NHS, financial services, or any client with a formal procurement or due diligence process.
follow_up_questions: Have you gone through a formal procurement or supplier vetting process with any client?
Have you been asked to complete a supplier questionnaire, security assessment, or tender?
Do you have cyber essentials certification or similar?
Do you carry professional indemnity or cyber liability insurance?
What is the typical size or type of organisation you sell to?
focus_areas.output_intro: Here's what enterprise procurement teams typically check — and how you currently measure up:

focus_areas.label: Learner Data Protection
focus_areas.short_desc: Are you handling learner personal data (progress, scores, identifiers) in a way that satisfies enterprise HR and legal teams?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you run an LMS, deliver online learning, or collect any data about learners during training.
follow_up_questions: Which platform or LMS do you use to deliver learning? (e.g. Moodle, TalentLMS, Articulate Rise, custom)
What learner data do you collect? (e.g. completion status, quiz scores, time-on-task, video watch progress)
Who can access individual learner data — just your team, the learner, or the client employer too?
How long do you retain learner records after a programme ends?
Do learners create accounts with your platform, or are they provisioned by the client?
Do any AI tools you use interact with or assess learners directly?
focus_areas.output_intro: Enterprise HR and legal teams ask specific questions about learner data. Here's how your current setup holds up:

focus_areas.label: AI Disclosure & Governance
focus_areas.short_desc: Are you transparent about how you use AI — with buyers, with learners, and in your contracts?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you use AI tools in content creation, delivery, or your business operations — or if clients have started asking about AI.
follow_up_questions: Which AI tools does your team use in designing or delivering learning? (e.g. ChatGPT, Copilot, Midjourney, AI authoring tools)
Does any AI tool interact directly with learners — e.g. AI tutor, chatbot, adaptive engine?
Is AI used to generate or significantly influence any learning content?
Are learners told when AI is involved in their learning or assessment?
Do your client contracts or terms of service mention AI use?
Have any enterprise clients asked you to complete an AI supplier questionnaire?
focus_areas.output_intro: Enterprise clients are increasingly asking L&D suppliers about AI. Here's how your current transparency measures up:

focus_areas.label: Cookie & Consent Compliance
focus_areas.short_desc: Does your consent mechanism actually work — and would it hold up to a GDPR audit?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you've been flagged for cookie issues, use analytics or marketing trackers, or want to verify your consent banner is genuinely compliant.
follow_up_questions: Which consent management platform (CMP) do you currently use?
Do you use Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, LinkedIn Insight Tag, or similar marketing trackers?
Has your CMP ever been flagged or complained about by a visitor?
Does your consent banner block all non-essential cookies until consent is given?
Do you have a record of consent that you could produce if audited?
focus_areas.output_intro: Here's what Papaya's live browser agent found when it visited your site — and what it means in practice:

focus_areas.label: Terms, Contracts & DPAs
focus_areas.short_desc: Do you have the legal documents enterprise clients will ask for before signing?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you're pitching to enterprise clients, have been asked for a DPA, or want to make sure your contractual setup is ready for serious buyers.
follow_up_questions: Do you have a standard set of terms and conditions you use with clients?
Have any clients asked you to sign their DPA, or asked you to provide yours?
Do you have a sub-processor list you can share?
Are any of your clients in regulated sectors (healthcare, financial services, education, government)?
Do you have a solicitor or data protection advisor who has reviewed your contracts?
focus_areas.output_intro: Enterprise procurement almost always requires specific legal documents before a contract is issued. Here's where you stand:

focus_areas.label: Technical Security
focus_areas.short_desc: Do your security headers and configuration pass the checks IT and security teams run on suppliers?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you've been asked to complete a security questionnaire, are working with clients in regulated sectors, or want to prepare for Cyber Essentials.
follow_up_questions: Have you or your hosting provider done a security review of your site in the last 12 months?
Do you have Cyber Essentials or any other security certification?
Does your site include any user authentication (login areas, portals)?
Do you use a CDN (e.g. Cloudflare) that might allow easy header configuration?
focus_areas.output_intro: Here's how your site's security configuration compares to what IT teams check in supplier reviews:

focus_areas.label: First Impressions & Buyer Trust Signals
focus_areas.short_desc: What does a buyer see when they google you before a meeting — and does it build or break confidence?
focus_areas.why_select: Choose this if you want to understand the basic trust signals buyers check before replying to your outreach or agreeing to a meeting.
follow_up_questions: What does your target buyer typically google about you before a first call?
Do you know if any buyers have visited your site and then not responded to outreach?
Is there any specific concern — e.g. no testimonials, no clear pricing, no case studies — that you think affects trust?
focus_areas.output_intro: 4 in 5 enterprise buyers check your website before responding to outreach. Here's what they currently find:
